title: Dark Molten Filipino Pork Dinuguan
description: A traditional Filipino dish with a twist, featuring biryani masala and a molten texture, served in a tagliere vessel. This sugar-free-friendly recipe is perfect for dinner.
instructions: Heat a large tagliere vessel over medium heat on a yakimono-grill.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the garlic and onion and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the biryani masala, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the pork blood and vinegar, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally to prevent scorching.
Serve the dinuguan hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
